_comment: Tunables for how the companion FEELS. Distances are in pixels, speeds in px/sec, intervals in seconds. 'personality' is the legacy 0..1 fallback; 'traits' are the live, drifting versions seeded from it. 'drift' controls how slowly the companion comes to reflect how you play. Tune all of this to change the vibe without touching code.
_follow_comment: The companion's comfortable distance TIGHTENS as the bond grows. Fresh, it keeps a berth as wide as the whole map (follow_near_low) — its own creature, free to be anywhere, with NO pull to trail you; the camera is locked to the player, so a fresh companion will often be off-screen, living its own life. As you bond this shrinks to the snug at-your-side follow_near. The map spans about 2220x1680px (a ~2784px diagonal), so follow_near_low/follow_far at map scale mean the leash effectively never reels in a fresh companion anywhere on the map — only a more-bonded one. run_distance is the separate walk->run threshold: how far behind the companion must fall before it hustles to catch up (so a bonded companion can still keep up with a moving player).
_wander_comment: Self-directed roaming — the independent, pre-bond life. Left alone the companion moseys to a spot, lingers, then after a pause picks somewhere new. wander_radius: how near a prop must be to be chosen as a destination (it investigates props it passes). roam_radius: how far an aimless amble strays when there's no prop to head for — set to map scale so a fresh companion can end up anywhere on the map (it shrinks on its own as the bond grows). roam_pause: [min,max] seconds of standing about between roams. roam_pause_bond_scale: pauses are multiplied toward this as bond->1, so a bonded companion ranges less. wander_linger: seconds it dwells once it arrives.
_bond_comment: The relationship itself (0..1), persistent across sessions. It starts at 'init' (a fresh companion is its own creature) and grows: grow_per_sec always while present, grow_per_sec_near extra while you stay within the (bond-scaled) comfort distance, grow_per_interaction on each shared examine. The base rates are calibrated for the REAL game: continuous play reaches a fairly close bond in roughly 10 hours. 'time_scale' multiplies all growth so you can experience the full fresh->bonded transformation quickly while tuning feel — at 145 a full bond takes about 5 minutes. Set time_scale back to 1.0 for the real game. Bond is saved, so a returning companion already knows you.
_balance_comment: How bond tips the wander-vs-follow contest. Self-wander scores wander_score_low at bond 0 (its own agenda) fading to wander_score_high at bond 1. Following scores follow_eager_low..follow_eager_high by bond, PLUS a distance leash (0 at the comfort distance ramping to follow_leash at follow_far). follow_eager_low is 0 and the comfort distance is map-wide when fresh, so a new companion has no pull to follow anywhere on the map; the leash only comes to matter as the bond deepens and the comfort distance tightens in. A player-triggered look is fixed well above all of these.

traits._comment: init must match personality above so first-run feel is unchanged. min/max bound how far a trait can drift; drift_rate is how fast (per second toward target) — keep it small so change is felt, not seen.

drift._comment: warmup_seconds: watch this long before drifting at all. reference_* set what counts as 'a lot' of roaming / examining. targets: weighted blend of play signals (explore, together, engage) each trait drifts toward.
